It belongs to the genus Tithymalus, a group of plants that are typically characterized by their milky sap and small, greenish flowers. The species name 'mesopotamicus' suggests a geographical origin in the region of Mesopotamia, but the exact distribution of this plant is not widely documented. The classification of Tithymalus mesopotamicus reflects its close relationship with other species in the Euphorbiaceae family. Further research is needed to determine the exact classification and distribution of this plant.

Tithymalus mesopotamicus, or Euphorbia species, is a succulent plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its green stems and white latex. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as wound healing, skin infections, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus mesopotamicus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
